summary: Deep-research the 2025/2026 best-practice + open-source landscape for the consolidation/baskets arena (I-cons-001 #1295)

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Claimify (Metropolitansky & Larson, Microsoft)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: 4-stage LLM claim extraction: Sentence-Splitting -> Selection (keep only sentences with verifiable propositions, drop opinion/speculation) -> Disambiguation (resolve referential/structural ambiguity, DROP a sentence if ambiguity cannot be confidently resolved) -> Decomposition (rewrite each clarified sentence into atomic, decontextualized, self-contained claims). The disambiguation gate is the novel part: it refuses to emit a claim it cannot confidently ground, which directly matches POLARIS's clinical-safety 'never fabricate' posture.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: ACL 2025 main conference; the reference method for extracting verifiable, decontextualized claims from LLM/long-form text. Outperforms prior decomposition on the BingCheck benchmark on coverage + entailment-to-source. Its 'extract only when ambiguity is resolved' rule is the SOTA answer to the over-/under-decomposition failure that pure-regex extraction cannot handle.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: Paper CC-BY-4.0; NO official code from Microsoft (method-only)

best_practice_2025_2026.name: CORE (Jiang et al.) — decompose-and-dedup to a non-redundant core
best_practice_2025_2026.what: The decompose-AND-dedup canonicalization piece this facet is about. Takes subclaims from any decomposer and (1) filters claims not entailed by the source (faithfulness), (2) scores informativeness, (3) DEDUPLICATES via Mixed-Integer Linear Programming, selecting the smallest set of mutually-non-entailing (= non-redundant) atomic claims. Output is a canonical 'core' claim set — exactly the dedup-ready atoms POLARIS needs before basket clustering.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Formalizes claim canonicalization/dedup as constrained optimization rather than ad-hoc string matching; the de-facto 'dedup half' of the Decompose-Then-Verify paradigm. Semantic (entailment-based) uniqueness instead of POLARIS's current lexical numeric-signature/shingle-Jaccard. MIT-licensed and runs on local RoBERTa-NLI or a vLLM OpenAI-compatible endpoint => sovereign.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: MedScore — domain-adapted clinical claim decomposition
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Decomposes free-form MEDICAL answers into verifiable claims with a clinical-domain-adapted decomposition prompt (respects symptom/diagnosis/treatment/contraindication claim structures that generic FActScore-style decomposition mis-parses). Emits one (sentence_id, claim, claim_id) record per atomic claim — the structured, dedup-ready shape.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Accepted ACL 2026 (publish 2026-07-02); the single most on-target frontier item for a sovereign CLINICAL pipeline. Directly replaces POLARIS's hardcoded clinical-regex endpoint vocabulary with a generalizable, model-agnostic clinical decomposer. Repo supports locally-hosted vLLM / HuggingFace models (sovereign), not only closed APIs.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: Repo license unconfirmed on GitHub (LICENSE file not surfaced) — VERIFY before adoption; default config uses closed gpt-4o-mini but vLLM/HF backends supported

best_practice_2025_2026.name: DnDScore — Decontextualization + Decomposition for factuality
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Studies the interaction of DECOMPOSITION and DECONTEXTUALIZATION: a decomposed atom must be made context-independent (resolve pronouns/ellipsis to the full entity) so it is checkable and dedup-comparable in isolation, but evaluated WITH the decontextualized context to avoid losing the qualifier that distinguishes two near-identical claims (e.g. timepoint/population).
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: EMNLP 2025 main. The canonical treatment of decontextualization for atomic claims — the exact axis POLARIS's lexical canonicalization misses (it folds DOI/title but does not semantically decontextualize the claim text). Cautions that naive pronoun-replacement does not always help, which guards against over-canonicalization that would merge distinct clinical claims.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: The Alignment Bottleneck in Decomposition-Based Claim Verification
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Diagnostic 2026 result: decomposition can HURT when decomposed atoms drift from the source claim's intent/structure ('alignment bottleneck'). Progress depends less on how aggressively you split and more on keeping every atom faithful to its source span.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Feb-2026 frontier caution that re-centers the goal: SOTA atomic extraction is span-faithful decomposition, not maximal splitting. Validates POLARIS's atom-first, span-grounded design (literal_text + span offsets) as the right architecture and warns against an unconstrained LLM decomposer that loses source fidelity.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: VeriScore — verifiable-claim extractor (open-weight)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Three-step factuality pipeline whose stage 1 is a claim EXTRACTOR fine-tuned to pull only VERIFIABLE claims (not every sentence), shipped as an open-weight Mistral-based model. Distinguishes verifiable vs unverifiable content before decomposition — reduces junk atoms entering the basket layer.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: EMNLP 2024 Findings; the practical OSS baseline that fixes FActScore's 'extract everything' over-generation. Apache-2.0, pip-installable, fine-tuned extractor on HuggingFace (SYX/mistral_based_claim_extractor) runs fully locally => sovereign for the extraction step (only its Serper retrieval is non-sovereign, which POLARIS already replaces with its own retrieval).

open_source_solutions.what_it_does: Decompose-then-dedup-to-core: entailment filter + informativeness scorer + MILP deduplication to a minimal non-redundant atomic-claim set. Backends: local RoBERTa-NLI (Zhengping/roberta-large-unli) OR vLLM OpenAI-compatible. SOVEREIGN-capable.
open_source_solutions.maturity: Low adoption (~8 stars, 18 commits) but actively maintained with CI; comprehensive docs + FActScore integration example. Research-grade, integratable.

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS has TWO atom-extraction code paths; only one is LIVE. LIVE: src/polaris_graph/generator/claim_atom_extractor.py (I-gen-005) — a pure-Python, deterministic, NO-LLM REGEX extractor (module docstring lines 48-51 'INTENTIONALLY pure-Python regex — no LLM call'). It produces structured ClaimAtom records (claim_atom_extractor.py:65-99: entity/endpoint/comparator/timepoint/value/unit + span_start/span_end + literal_text) with a NumberRole classifier (lines 107-116) and a HARDCODED clinical endpoint vocabulary (_ENDPOINT_VOCAB lines 182-302: HbA1c, MACE, eGFR...), drug list (lines 326-345) and trial list (lines 347-367). It is imported by the live generator path: multi_section_generator.py, evidence_distiller.py, sentinel_contract.py, atom_refusal_validator.py (grep confirmed). LEGACY/DEAD on the live path: src/utils/atomic_decomposer.py — an LLM decomposer (Min et al. 2023 FActScore-style) but its initialize() imports KIMIClient then falls back to GeminiClient (atomic_decomposer.py:125-137; Gemini = §9.1.8 sovereignty violation); only consumer is src/agents/auditor_agent.py (pipeline-C legacy). CANONICALIZATION/DEDUP (consolidation side) is entirely LEXICAL, not semantic: fact_dedup.py builds a numeric FactSignature (percent/dollar-±5%-bucket/year, lines 252-305) and groups by overlap (build_groups, _signatures_overlap lines 532-600), plus an OFF-by-default prose pass using content-word shingle Jaccard >=0.82 + a polarity guard (_build_prose_groups lines 450-507, PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE). benchmark/claim_dedup.py does Claimify-CITED dedup but again lexical: numeric-signature-conflict + content-token Jaccard>=0.80 + subject-token swap guard (dedup_claims lines 162-194). synthesis/finding_dedup.py consolidates by a conservative finding key (subject/predicate/value/unit/dose/arm/endpoint, _finding_key lines 381-412) and same-work DOI/folded-title folding (lines 316-336) — keep-all corroboration, NOT atomic-claim canonicalization.
facets.gap_vs_frontier: Two-axis gap. (1) EXTRACTION: POLARIS's live extractor is REGEX with a hardcoded clinical vocabulary (claim_atom_extractor.py:182-367). It cannot extract any out-of-vocabulary claim, any qualitative/non-numeric claim, or any non-clinical-domain claim (the §-1.3 'generalize off clinical' need), and has no Selection/Disambiguation gate — the frontier (Claimify) uses LLM semantic decomposition that (a) keeps only verifiable propositions, (b) DROPS claims whose ambiguity cannot be resolved, and (c) decontextualizes each atom. The only LLM decomposer in-tree (atomic_decomposer.py) is dead AND non-sovereign (Gemini fallback). (2) CANONICALIZATION/DEDUP: POLARIS dedup is purely LEXICAL — numeric-signature buckets + shingle/content-token Jaccard + DOI/title folding. The frontier is SEMANTIC: DnDScore decontextualization (resolve each atom to a context-free, comparable form) + CORE's entailment-based, MILP dedup-to-non-redundant-core. Lexical Jaccard cannot tell that 'tirzepatide cut HbA1c by 2.3 points' and 'A1C fell 2.3% with Mounjaro' are the SAME atomic claim (different surface, same fact) — so equivalent phrasings that should land in ONE basket stay split, fragmenting corroboration. NUANCE from 2026 (Alignment Bottleneck, 2602.10380): the fix is NOT maximal LLM splitting — it is SPAN-FAITHFUL decomposition; POLARIS's existing span-grounding (literal_text + offsets) is the right substrate to bolt a faithful LLM decomposer onto, and CORE's entailment filter is in-scope as a PRE-verify canonicalizer (distinct from the frozen strict_verify/NLI/4-role engine).
bakeoff_candidates: Claimify 4-stage decomposition (re-implement in-house against arXiv 2502.10855 on the sovereign GLM/DeepSeek slate — Selection+Disambiguation+Decomposition+decontextualize) vs the live pure-regex claim_atom_extractor.py, scored on extraction coverage + span-faithfulness on a banked clinical corpus_snapshot

CORE (MIT, zipJiang/Core) entailment+MILP dedup-to-non-redundant-core as the SEMANTIC canonicalizer feeding the basket layer, vs current lexical fact_dedup/claim_dedup Jaccard — measure how many equivalent-phrasing atoms correctly collapse into ONE basket WITHOUT over-merging distinct clinical claims (§-1.1 lethal direction). NOTE: use only CORE's decompose+dedup halves; its entailment step must NOT replace the frozen strict_verify/NLI/4-role engine

MedScore (Heyuan9/MedScore, ACL 2026, vLLM-sovereign — VERIFY repo license first) clinical-domain-adapted decomposer as the drop-in replacement for the hardcoded _ENDPOINT_VOCAB regex, on clinical free-form text
VeriScore open-weight Mistral extractor (Apache-2.0) as a verifiable-vs-unverifiable PRE-filter before decomposition, to keep opinion/unverifiable text out of baskets

DnDScore decontextualization pass (EMNLP 2025) layered on each atom before dedup, to make equivalent phrasings comparable while preserving the qualifier (timepoint/population) that must keep distinct claims separate

Hybrid baseline: keep the live regex extractor for high-precision numeric clinical atoms + add an LLM semantic decomposer (on the sovereign slate) for qualitative/out-of-vocab/non-clinical claims, dedup-canonicalized by CORE — measured against the regex-only and LLM-only ends

best_practice_2025_2026.name: MultiClaimNet — embedding + agglomerative same-claim clustering (bge-m3)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: First dedicated same-claim CLUSTER benchmark + method: embed each claim with a multilingual sentence-transformer (bge-m3 best), then agglomerative clustering at a distance threshold groups claims asserting the SAME underlying fact. Cluster membership is keep-all (every corroborating claim stays in the cluster). Pairwise 'same claim?' gold labels were produced by 7 LLMs (GPT-4/Phi-3-14B/Falcon-40B unanimous).
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Best result on the 85.3K-claim MultiClaim set: bge-m3 + agglomerative = 0.574 ARI / 0.967 V-measure. Establishes the 2025 SOTA recipe for basketing (semantic embedding clustering, not surface keys), and is exactly the consolidate-keep-all primitive POLARIS needs.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: dataset on Zenodo (15100352); annotation via open + closed LLMs — verify Zenodo license before reuse

best_practice_2025_2026.name: SemEval-2025 Task 7 — fact-checked claim retrieval / matching SOTA encoders
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Multilingual+crosslingual previously-fact-checked claim retrieval (claim-matching = 'can these two claims be verified by the same evidence'). Bi-encoder dense retrievers fine-tuned for claim similarity are the workhorse; the canonical 'are these the same claim' decision.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Top systems: multilingual-e5-large-instruct (63.23 avg), NVIDIA NV-Embed-v2 best in some configs. Notable caveat for clinical sovereignty: a BM25 lexical baseline beat SOTA multilingual embedders monolingually — argues for a HYBRID lexical+dense matcher, not dense-only.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: task data CC-BY (verify); e5 models MIT, NV-Embed-v2 CC-BY-NC (non-sovereign, FLAG)

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Qwen3-Embedding-8B — MTEB #1 multilingual encoder for the clustering primitive
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Foundation-model text embedding (0.6B/4B/8B) used as the vectorizer feeding agglomerative/community-detection clustering. Instruction-tunable embeddings let you steer 'cluster by same factual claim'.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: #1 on MTEB multilingual (70.58), MTEB clustering 57.65 (8B). Apache-2.0, sovereign-deployable via sentence-transformers/vLLM. POLARIS already chose Qwen3-Embedding-8B in I-arch-009 (#1266) for the quality gate — reuse it as the basketing vectorizer.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Claimify — decontextualized atomic-claim extraction (canonical claim units)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: 3-stage LLM pipeline (Selection → Disambiguation → Decomposition) producing specific, verifiable, DECONTEXTUALIZED atomic claims. Decontextualization is the missing pre-step that makes two paraphrases of the same fact cluster reliably (resolves pronouns/ellipsis before matching).
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Outperforms prior extraction; the decontextualized claim is the unit MultiClaimNet/VeriScore cluster over. Directly addresses POLARIS's prose-claim under-clustering (literal Jaccard can't match paraphrases).
best_practice_2025_2026.license: Microsoft Research method; community OSS reimpls (MIT-ish) — no official MS release, FLAG

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS basketing is DETERMINISTIC key-equality + exact-Jaccard, with NO embedding/NLI semantic clustering for grouping. (1) synthesis/finding_dedup.py:381-412 `_finding_key` groups rows by an exact tuple (subject/predicate/value/unit/dose/arm/endpoint) from the clinical-tuned `extract_numeric_claims`; under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN it KEEPS ALL same-claim rows as a basket (the collapse-drop is bypassed, finding_dedup.py:737-789, collapsed_row_count->0) and counts corroboration over independent registrable-domains (finding_dedup.py:696-712). Same-work duplicates (same DOI / folded-title+discriminator) consolidate via `consolidate_same_work` (finding_dedup.py:316-336,455-546) so 4 URLs of one Autor paper = ONE origin. Documented RESIDUAL 2 (finding_dedup.py:29-41): INERT on non-clinical numerics. (2) synthesis/claim_graph.py is the field-agnostic upgrade -- atomic-claim extraction + stance clustering by SHA-1 of a conservative normalized key (claim_graph.py:33-47,112-119) with an INJECTED NLI judge -- but NLI is used only for conflict/refute EDGES, not same-claim grouping, and it is DEFAULT-OFF (PG_SWEEP_CLAIM_GRAPH). (3) generator/fact_dedup.py:666-768 build_groups clusters numeric sentences by FactSignature overlap and prose sentences by exact content-word shingle Jaccard>=0.82 (fact_dedup.py:381-507) with a deterministic polarity/negation guard (fact_dedup.py:433-447). generator/weighted_enrichment.py surfaces basket `supporting_members` with span_verdict==SUPPORTS, ordered by relevance then weight_mass, keep-all-sort-below-floor-last (weighted_enrichment.py:222-265). Net: grouping is literal/numeric only -- corroboration is computed correctly once items are in a basket, but two PARAPHRASES of the same prose claim never land in the same basket.
facets.gap_vs_frontier: POLARIS groups same-claim by LITERAL keys: exact numeric-signature equality (finding_dedup `_finding_key`, fact_dedup FactSignature) or exact content-word shingle Jaccard (fact_dedup >=0.82). The 2025/2026 frontier groups by SEMANTIC equivalence -- embedding clustering (MultiClaimNet: bge-m3 + agglomerative, 0.574 ARI) and/or NLI bi-directional-entailment / LLM 'same claim?' confirm -- over DECONTEXTUALIZED atomic claims (Claimify/VeriScore). Three concrete gaps: (1) PARAPHRASE BLINDNESS -- a prose claim restated in different words (no shared %/$/year, Jaccard<0.82) forms its OWN singleton basket, so corroboration is UNDER-counted exactly where the weight-and-consolidate DNA needs it (qualitative clinical claims: 'not recommended' vs 'should be avoided' vs 'contraindicated' never cluster). (2) NON-CLINICAL INERTNESS -- finding_dedup's clinical-tuned numeric extractor yields no key for economics/policy numerics (RESIDUAL 2), so those corroborators don't basket; claim_graph would fix this but is DEFAULT-OFF and unused. (3) NLI is wired only for CONFLICT edges, never for same-claim EQUIVALENCE grouping -- the frontier's core basketing primitive (bidirectional entailment = same claim) is absent. The faithfulness engine (FROZEN) is untouched by all of this -- basketing is a credibility WEIGHT, so adding semantic clustering only raises corroboration recall, never relaxes a verify gate. Caveat from SemEval-2025: dense-only under-performs BM25 monolingually, so the right design is a HYBRID lexical+dense candidate-gen + a precision NLI/LLM 'same claim?' confirm to preserve POLARIS's conservative-singleton (never over-merge) invariant.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Uncertainty-Aware Web-Conditioned Scientific Fact-Checking (Vinod & Erk)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: The single closest published match to POLARIS's whole basket pipeline IN ITS NATIVE DOMAIN (scientific/biomedical fact-checking): atomic predicate-argument decomposition -> a compact evidence-grounded per-fact checker -> UNCERTAINTY-GATED multi-source web corroboration (consult external sources ONLY when calibrated confidence is low) -> abstain-with-NEI rather than override the context when evidence conflicts. The corroboration step is gated by calibrated confidence, which is exactly the calibrated-basket-confidence + label-don't-silently-override posture POLARIS approximates.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: PRIMARY-SOURCE VERIFIED (title/authors Ashwin Vinod & Katrin Erk / date 2026-04-13 / CC-BY-4.0 confirmed by fetch). It is more directly the SOTA-comparator and prior-art for corroboration-weighting than any crowd-voting or news-credibility paper reasoned onto baskets by analogy, because its native task IS evidence-source corroboration for high-stakes clinical fact-checking. Pair it with the OSS picks as the design reference for uncertainty-GATED (not always-on) corroboration.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: CC BY 4.0 (paper); code release not confirmed on the abstract page

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Reliability-aware iterative truth discovery (Dawid-Skene / M-MSR / CRH family) via crowd-kit
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Jointly estimate per-source RELIABILITY and the consensus truth in one EM loop: a source that agrees with high-weight sources gains weight; a value asserted by high-weight sources is more likely true. This is the weight<->consensus feedback POLARIS lacks (its source weight is a fixed authority prior; its consensus is a raw independent-origin count). Maps onto baskets as: members=sources, claim value=label, credibility=worker reliability.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: The 2015 truth-discovery concept (TruthFinder/CRH, arXiv:1505.02463) is FOUNDATIONAL not current; what makes it 2025/2026-current is the maintained crowd-kit implementation (PRIMARY-SOURCE VERIFIED: v1.4.2 Oct-2025, Apache-2.0, scikit-learn fit_predict API, Dawid-Skene/M-MSR/GLAD/MACE/Wawa). Frame as '2015 concept realized via the current crowd-kit impl', never 'TruthFinder is the current method'. Gives a reliability-aware credibility-weighted consensus value to replace the flat origin COUNT.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Beyond Majority Voting: Optimal Weight / Inverse Surprising Popularity (Ai, Pan, Simchi-Levi, Tambe, Xu)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Aggregate multiple answers by assigning DIFFERENTIATED weights from first- AND second-order information (latent heterogeneity + correlation across answerers), provably beating majority voting. Applied BY ANALOGY to baskets it would upgrade POLARIS's BINARY copy-or-origin independence to a CONTINUOUS correlation-aware down-weight, so two partially-redundant non-copy sources count as less than two full independent origins.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: PRIMARY-SOURCE VERIFIED and DOMAIN-CAVEAT VERIFIED: its native task is aggregating multiple LLM ANSWERS (datasets MMLU, UltraFeedback, ARMMAN), NOT evidence-source corroboration. It is a legitimate pattern TRANSFER for the correlation-continuum idea, but must be labeled domain-mismatched (model-vote aggregation), NOT crowned 'the SOTA primitive for weighting corroborators'. v2 rev 2026-05-19; ICML-2026-class.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Bayesian Beta-Binomial calibrated basket confidence + scikit-learn calibration backbone
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Turn a basket into a CALIBRATED probability + credible interval: combine per-source agreement evidence with source-reliability WEIGHTS into a Beta posterior (unreliable sources down-weight the update); then score the calibration with Brier/ECE. Replaces POLARIS's log1p-of-a-count strength signal (compute_signal_d) and its 4-value basket_verdict LABEL with a probability the existing calibration_metrics.py can verify.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: The principled, sovereign, fully-OSS path to a calibrated basket confidence (scikit-learn BSD-3 CalibratedClassifierCV/calibration_curve + Brier). POLARIS already SHIPS the offline scorer (calibration_metrics.py Brier/ECE/MCE) but with NO production caller — this wires the proof loop. The Beta-Binomial posterior is a small deterministic update reimplementable from standard Bayesian sources.

Pure-stdlib Python, fully reproducible, no NewsGuard/MBFC API. A deterministic auditable DOWN-weight for known-problematic news/web/social domains.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: PRIMARY-SOURCE VERIFIED (arXiv:2604.20856, github.com/aloth/cred-1, CC-BY-4.0, v1.0 Feb-2026). CRITICAL NUANCE the prior run missed: it is a NEGATIVE-signal dataset — it lists ONLY domains with known credibility ISSUES; a domain ABSENT from CRED-1 must be treated as neutral/unknown, NOT reliable. So it can sharpen the §-1.3 'social/web stays low-weight' down-weighting of known-bad domains, but it can NEVER up-weight a source — wire it as a down-weight-only overlay, never as the authority prior.

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS has a deep, well-architected corroboration/weighting stack that is genuinely the §-1.3 weight-and-consolidate DNA in code, but it weights by FIXED authority priors and aggregates by a COUNT of independent origins — no reliability<->consensus feedback, no correlation continuum, no calibrated posterior. Grounded (every claim read in-repo): (1) synthesis/weight_mass.py:160-180 — cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin) ONLY; copies contribute ZERO (copy-uninflatable 'vax-defense', KBT arXiv:1502.03519); credibility_weight is CARRIED but DELIBERATELY NOT a mass factor (:161-167 comment proves folding it in breaks the no-inflation invariant). ADVISORY, default-OFF PG_SWEEP_WEIGHT_MASS (:36-42 — zero production callers, pure library). (2) synthesis/credibility_pass.py:209-233,611-762 — per-claim ClaimBasket keeps ALL supporting_members (never drops, §-1.3); verified_support_origin_count (the only strengthening count) = DISTINCT origin clusters whose member passed ISOLATED single-token per-member span verify (_verify_member_in_isolation:341-388, anti-laundering); basket_verdict is a LABEL only (full/partial/contested/unverified, :161-164) from counts + refuter edges, never resurrects a dropped sentence. (3) authority/corroboration.py:58-80 — corroboration_count = count_independent_hosts (eTLD+1 registrable domains via PSL); compute_signal_d turns it into a log1p sub-score (the closest thing to a consensus-confidence, but a monotone COUNT transform, not agreement- or reliability-weighted). (4) generator/weighted_enrichment.py:286-329 — surfaces the FULL ordered unbound-SUPPORTS basket (no cap, below-floor sorted-last-not-dropped, B18) through the UNCHANGED strict_verify; generator/fact_dedup.py same-work (DOI/title+discriminator) consolidation so N URLs of one paper = ONE source. (5) synthesis/calibration_metrics.py:79-129 — Brier/ECE/MCE/reliability-bins EXIST but PURE offline, NO production caller. The frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance) is the only hard gate and is untouched — everything above is ADVISORY weight/label. FLAG-SPLIT (verified): the SAME flag PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN defaults ON in synthesis/selection (credibility_pass.py:118 getenv(...,'on')) but OFF in retrieval (live_retriever.py:379 in-set with empty default) — so on an unset/default run the down-weight-don't-drop redesign is LIVE in synthesis but the retriever's content-starved rows still HARD-DROP (split-brain, same class as the I-arch-007 602->22 funnel).
facets.gap_vs_frontier: Five concrete gaps, all in ADVISORY weight/consensus/confidence layers, none touching the frozen faithfulness engine: (1) NO reliability-aware truth-discovery — POLARIS source weight = a fixed authority prior (credibility_skill capped at clamp01(authority)+uplift); the frontier (crowd-kit Dawid-Skene/M-MSR, concept arXiv:1505.02463) iterates weight<->consensus so a source's weight reflects its AGREEMENT with other credible sources, not just its venue. (2) Correlation is BINARY — independence_collapse is copy-or-origin (TF-IDF cosine>=0.85 + host-collapse); the OW/ISP continuum (2510.01499, DOMAIN-TRANSFERRED) would partially down-weight two partially-redundant non-copy sources POLARIS counts as two full independent origins. (3) Consensus strength is a COUNT, not a credibility-weighted aggregate — verified_support_origin_count and compute_signal_d are origin counts / log1p-of-a-count; the credibility_weight POLARIS computes is REFUSED entry into the strength signal (by design, to protect no-inflation — but a credibility-weighted consensus a la reliability-discovery, or the confidence-weighted-consensus / Roundtable-Policy pattern arXiv:2509.16839 which the facet scope literally names under 'consensus / weighted voting', would be more discriminative WITHOUT inflation if it weights members already in the basket, never adds count). (4) NO calibrated basket confidence — basket_verdict is a 4-value label; no Beta-Binomial/Bayesian posterior turning a basket into a calibrated probability + credible interval (the native pattern in arXiv:2604.11036's uncertainty-GATED corroboration). (5) calibration_metrics.py is UNWIRED — the Brier/ECE machinery that would PROVE basket confidences are calibrated has zero production caller. PLUS a verified repo split-brain (flag default ON in synthesis, OFF in retrieval) the prior run only half-corrected. NUANCE: arXiv:2604.11036 (Vinod & Erk, 2026) is the missing NATIVE-domain prior-art the facet should compare against; the facet's headline pick 2510.01499 is model-vote aggregation transferred by analogy and must be demoted from 'the SOTA primitive' to 'domain-transferred pattern'. CRED-1 is NEGATIVE-signal (down-weight known-bad only, never up-weight; absent=neutral). Sovereignty: POLARIS authority priors lean journal/gov; CRED-1 (CC BY 4.0) gives news/web/social a transparent offline down-weight without a NewsGuard/MBFC API.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: ConflictRAG (two-stage conflict detection + Entropy-TOPSIS source arbitration + CARS metric)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Detects knowledge conflicts AMONG retrieved sources (source-vs-source, the exact intra-basket problem) with a 2-stage detector: a lightweight embedding-based MLP classifier first, then selective LLM refinement only on hard cases (62% API-cost reduction, 90.8% detection accuracy). Adds an Entropy-TOPSIS data-driven source-credibility arbitration (+7.1% selection accuracy over manual heuristics) and a Conflict-Aware RAG Score (CARS) for diagnostic eval.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Most recent (May 17 2026, rev Jun 8 2026) and directly on-point: it is source-vs-source conflict + a credibility-weighted arbitration, which is precisely POLARIS's basket-faithfulness verdict need. The cheap-classifier-first / LLM-only-on-hard-pairs design is the cost-and-sovereignty lever POLARIS's currently-capped LLM-NLI pass lacks.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: License unconfirmed (arXiv distribution license only; no code/license declared on the abstract page)

MIT-licensed and runnable fully offline — it can judge EVERY basket pair with no API spend and no socket-hang class, unlike a remote LLM judge.

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS already runs a THREE-detector intra-basket conflict stack feeding a claim-graph and a both-sides composer — this facet is well-built, the gap is wiring/sovereignty, not absence. (1) NUMERIC: contradiction_detector.py — deterministic regex (subject,predicate,value,unit), >10% rel-diff flags ContradictionRecord (:480-507, incl. the A17 not_comparable commensurability guard :494-507); disclose-both, never drop; B9 generic non-clinical path. (2) QUALITATIVE: qualitative_conflict_detector.py:1-27 — NegEx/ConText rule-cue PRESENT/ABSENT 2-pass (hard conflict + review_flag), NO LLM, default ON (:94-97). (3) SEMANTIC NLI: semantic_conflict_detector.py — LLM-NLI judge default z-ai/glm-5.2 (:69), cluster-by-salient-words -> pairs -> CONTRADICT/ENTAIL/NEUTRAL conf>=0.7, but DEFAULT OFF (:222) and COST-CAPPED at MAX_PAIRS=60 / MAX_ROWS=200 (:63-65) with a conflict_unscored disclosed-gap label and strict_fail_closed (:325). These three feed claim_graph.py (imports all three as edge sources :58-71) which builds ContradictionEdge objects between claim CLUSTERS/baskets (:190, recall-first), and both_sides.py:68 compose_both_sides emits per-contested-cluster BothSidesBlocks ordered by Phase-6 origin-cluster weight_mass (never headcount, low-weight side kept not dropped), with dissent_recall_builder.py targeting the minority side. A within-basket stance primitive also already exists: fact_dedup.py:433 _polarity_signature blocks merging opposite-polarity prose (raised vs lowered, inserted 'not') at high Jaccard, so support/refute prose never silently consolidates. (Note: anti_sycophancy/stance_delta.py:45 classifies agree/disagree/hedge but for PROMPT-DRIFT across 4 framings, NOT basket stance — out of facet.)
facets.gap_vs_frontier: The machinery exists but is (a) NOT WIRED and (b) NOT SOVEREIGN/ALWAYS-ON. Three concrete deltas vs the frontier: (1) The semantic NLI edge source — the only detector that catches prose-only directional disagreement with no number and no NegEx cue (the F12 lethal-miss class) — is a REMOTE LLM (GLM-5.2), default OFF, and hard-capped to 60 judged pairs. The 2025/2026 frontier (ModernCE-large-nli, MIT, 8192-ctx, local) makes it feasible to run a deterministic NLI cross-encoder on EVERY basket pair with zero API spend, full sovereignty, and no socket-hang/cost-cap class — turning a capped, optional pass into an always-on basket attribute. (2) both_sides.py and dissent_recall_builder.py are DEFAULT-OFF pure libraries with NO production caller — the basket-level both-sides surfacing and minority-recall the task asks for is composed but not rendered into the actual report path. (3) No credibility-WEIGHTED structured stance verdict per basket: POLARIS has tier weights and weight_mass ordering, but unlike ConflictRAG's Entropy-TOPSIS it does not aggregate them into a single surfaced support/refute stance vector (count + summed authority weight per side + agreement) decorating the basket — the §-1.3 basket-faithfulness verdict is implied by the both-sides block, not emitted as a first-class structured attribute. Faithfulness engine stays untouched throughout (advisory/disclosure only).

best_practice_2025_2026.name: LLMxMapReduce (V1)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Map -> collapse -> reduce over a long multi-source input. Extracts a STRUCTURED INFORMATION unit per chunk; a structured information PROTOCOL handles inter-chunk DEPENDENCY and an IN-CONTEXT CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION mechanism resolves inter-chunk CONFLICT at reduce time. Model-agnostic. This is the direct upgrade target for POLARIS's REDUCE conflict handling (today plain contradiction_key string-equality).
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: ACL 2025 long paper; ablations show the structured protocol + confidence calibration let a divide-and-conquer pipeline beat representative OPEN and COMMERCIAL long-context LLMs, and it transfers across models.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: LLMxMapReduce-V2 (entropy-driven convolutional test-time scaling; SurveyGO)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Stacked 'convolutional' scaling layers progressively integrate hundreds of sources into one long grounded article, plus Best-of-N self-refinement. Purpose-built for long-form grounded report/survey generation from EXTREMELY LONG multi-source resources — the exact POLARIS multi-section grounded-report job. Ships the SurveyEval benchmark.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Current best OSS recipe for turning a large source set into one long grounded document; hierarchical (not flat) reduce, with a published survey-writing benchmark and a live system (SurveyGO).

best_practice_2025_2026.name: LLMxMapReduce-V3 (MCP-driven hierarchically-modular survey agent)

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Context-Aware Hierarchical Merging (CAHM)

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS already implements a real per-source-distill -> per-section-reduce map-reduce, gated behind PG_SECTION_DISTILL. (1) MAP: src/polaris_graph/generator/evidence_distiller.py — distill_section_evidence (evidence_distiller.py:1658) runs one LLM MAP call PER SOURCE (micro-batched 1..3, asyncio.Semaphore(parallel) fan-out, _max_parallel default 8 at :173), extracting atomic span-grounded DistilledFinding rows validated FAIL-CLOSED (_validate_finding :849: support_quote must locate in direct_quote via exact/whitespace/fuzzy recovery; fuzzy recoveries get a BLOCKING per-finding entailment via the unchanged verify_sentence_provenance; numbers-in-span telemetry-only/non-blocking per #1217). Every source yields >=1 finding OR a CoverageRow (LAW II, no silent drop). (2) REDUCE: a single-pass per-section compose over the VALIDATED_FINDINGS_LEDGER only (render_reduce_user :441, _REDUCE_SYSTEM :303), wired at multi_section_generator.py:3886 (distill_section_evidence) and :2743-2768 (section_reduce call); reducer never sees raw quotes, every sentence must carry a [[finding:]] marker + exact [ev_XXX] cite, uncited prose stripped (:4017 filter_and_strip_reduce_markers); strict_verify re-checks every reduced sentence (faithfulness engine FROZEN). (3) CONFLICT: _cluster_contradictions (evidence_distiller.py:1632) groups findings by exact-string contradiction_key when >=2 distinct sources share it — passed to REDUCE as framing only. (4) CROSS-SOURCE CONSOLIDATION is separate passes: finding_dedup.dedup_by_finding (synthesis/finding_dedup.py:575) clusters rows by numeric FINDING and (under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN) KEEPS ALL same-claim rows as a basket with corroboration_count + consolidate_same_work (:455, same-DOI/folded-title => one source, keep all URLs); generator/weighted_enrichment.py surfaces the full ordered SUPPORTS basket (ClaimBasket.supporting_members) with no cap; cross-section restatements handled by generator/fact_dedup.py build_groups/dedup_pass (numeric signature + #1289 prose-Jaccard, keep-all cross-ref rewrite). Scope is strictly PER-SECTION: section_title/section_focus thread through MAP and REDUCE (evidence_distiller.py:1687-1688), so there is NO single global multi-document reduce — sections compose independently then dedup.
facets.gap_vs_frontier: POLARIS is NOT missing map-reduce (it has one); the gap is the SHAPE of the reduce and the conflict protocol. (A) FLAT vs HIERARCHICAL reduce: POLARIS's REDUCE is a single-pass, per-section compose over a flat findings ledger (render_reduce_user / section_reduce); the 2025-2026 frontier (LLMxMapReduce-V2 entropy-driven convolutional scaling 2504.05732; V3 2510.10890; CAHM recursive context-aware merge 2502.00977) does ITERATIVE/HIERARCHICAL merge that preserves cross-source structure and salience as the corpus grows — on a wide corpus POLARIS's flat ledger -> single completion can lose cross-source interdependencies and starve coverage. (B) Cross-source SYNTHESIS is bolted on, not unified: POLARIS composes sections independently then runs separate dedup/basket passes (finding_dedup, fact_dedup, weighted_enrichment); there is no single global multi-document reduce producing 'one grounded answer' with cross-source relationships first-class, which the frontier provides. (C) CONFLICT handling is primitive: _cluster_contradictions uses exact-string contradiction_key equality (>=2 sources) fed as framing only; the frontier (LLMxMapReduce structured information protocol + in-context CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION) actually RESOLVES inter-source conflict at reduce time — POLARIS leaves resolution implicit and relies on the faithfulness gate to DROP, not reconcile. CONSTRAINT: every candidate slots in as the reduce/consolidation layer FEEDING the frozen strict_verify/NLI/4-role engine — none may resolve conflict in place of it. NOTE: POLARIS's basket/keep-all DNA (§-1.3) is AHEAD of vanilla map-reduce (which collapses duplicates); the right move keeps the basket consolidation and upgrades only the reduce shape + conflict protocol. License posture: every adoptable candidate is Apache-2.0 (LLMxMapReduce V1/V2/V3) or MIT (CAHM, LangGraph) — sovereignty-clean; XpandA is license-unverified (evidence only).

best_practice_2025_2026.name: DeepTRACE factual-support matrix (per-(statement,source) support, set-max aggregation)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Builds a Statement x Source binary 'factual-support matrix' via an LLM judge (GPT-5) scoring each (statement, source) pair independently; a statement is 'Unsupported' iff NO cited source supports it (max-over-the-cited-set), and Citation Accuracy = sum(CitationMtx (.) FactualSupportMtx)/sum(CitationMtx). This is the canonical 2025 definition of 'statement supported by its cited SET' and is one of POLARIS's two benchmark targets.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: arXiv paper (CC-BY); audit framework, GPT-5 judge is the reference impl (closed)
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Defines the field-standard set-based citation-faithfulness primitive and is POLARIS's headline DeepTRACE leaderboard target. Crucially its support model is per-source-then-max-over-set, i.e. the SAME shape POLARIS already uses (per-member isolation) — confirms POLARIS is aligned with the SOTA metric, not behind it. Honest limit: GPT-5 judge correlates only 0.62 Pearson with human labels.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: MedRAGChecker calibrated set-level support score P* (NLI(set) + KG, logistic fusion)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Clinical claim-level verifier: decomposes a long-form medical answer into atomic claims, verifies EACH claim against the WHOLE retrieved evidence set D (not per-chunk) by combining evidence-grounded NLI with biomedical knowledge-graph consistency signals, and fuses textual-NLI probability + KG support into ONE calibrated support score P* via logistic fusion; aggregates per-claim Entail/Neutral/Contradict into answer-level faithfulness / hallucination / safety-critical-error rates.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: paper; anonymous repo (license TBD at submission); GPT-4.1/4o teacher, open-weight distilled students (Meditron3-8B, Med42-Llama3-8B, Med-Qwen2-7B)
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: The clearest 2026 example of genuine SET-level (not per-span) basket faithfulness in the clinical domain: it aggregates the multi-evidence set INTO one calibrated confidence layered on NLI, plus a domain KG cross-check, and emits a safety-critical-error rate — exactly the advisory basket-confidence layer POLARIS lacks. Distilled open-weight students make it sovereign-portable.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Credibility-weighted multi-source aggregation with source-level disagreement + claim/negation calibration
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Retrieves multiple sources per claim, computes a verdict per source, then AGGREGATES them with explicit source-level-disagreement modeling and source-credibility weighting; runs both the claim AND its negation (dual perspective) and calibrates raw per-source confidence because LLM confidence is not comparable across models. Agreement across sources yields sharp high-confidence peaks; disagreement is surfaced rather than silently max-pooled.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Directly the basket-faithfulness frontier POLARIS aspires to in §-1.3: decide a claim against its WHOLE basket of corroborators, WEIGHTED by credibility, with disagreement made explicit and confidence CALIBRATED before combination. The claim+negation dual check is a concrete anti-one-sidedness mechanism that maps onto the DeepTRACE one-sided/overconfident metrics.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Natural-logic proof-based evidence-SET sufficiency (faithful-by-construction)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Generates a natural-logic proof (sequence of lexical mutations + NatLog operators) over the retrieved evidence to decide veracity AND whether the evidence SET is SUFFICIENT for the claim; the proof itself is the explanation, so sufficiency/veracity are 'faithful by construction' rather than a post-hoc score. Zero-shot LLM variants (2024-2025) remove the trained seq2seq dependency.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Provides a SET-level SUFFICIENCY verdict (is the basket enough to support the claim?) with a human-auditable proof — a property POLARIS's per-member-max model cannot produce. The proof artifact is a natural fit for POLARIS's signed-bundle / Proof-Replay UI and never launders fragments because each mutation step is span-anchored.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: AVeriTeC 2025 open-weights set-verdict pipelines (HerO 2 / CTU AIC) with explicit Conflicting/Cherry-picking label + Ev2R eval
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Decomposes a claim into question-answer evidence pairs, predicts veracity over the assembled QA-pair SET, and emits one of {Supported, Refuted, Not-Enough-Evidence, Conflicting-Evidence/Cherry-picking} — a set-level label that includes an explicit disagreement verdict. The 2025 shared task mandated open-weights, single-GPU (<=23GB), <=1-min systems, scored by Ev2R recall (LLM-based, reference-free).
best_practice_2025_2026.license: shared-task overview (ACL); HerO repo CC-BY-NC, FLAG; CTU AIC RAG pipeline
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Demonstrates a fully SOVEREIGN (open-weights, single-GPU, sub-minute) set-level veracity pipeline that produces a 'Conflicting Evidence / Cherry-picking' verdict — the exact output POLARIS needs when a basket's corroborators disagree. Sets the realistic compute/latency envelope for an advisory basket-aggregation layer on the VM.

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS does PER-MEMBER-ISOLATED entailment + CORROBORATION-AS-WEIGHT, NOT set-level joint verification. (1) Per-member isolation: credibility_pass._verify_member_in_isolation (credibility_pass.py:362-388) verifies each basket member ALONE against its OWN span. The anti-laundering invariant is explicit at L367-379: it strips any stray provenance/calc token and appends exactly ONE token spanning that member's own span ('With exactly one token the verifier's per-token union loop cannot aggregate this member's span with any other — a member whose own span lacks the claim fails ALONE'), and pool={eid: dict(member_row)} (L380) isolates the verifier to that single source. (2) span_verdict is strictly BINARY SUPPORTS/UNSUPPORTED (credibility_pass.py:167-200), and the per-member verify is ADVISORY — '_verify_member_in_isolation is never re-run as a gate' (L360, L418-420); on verifier exception it fails-closed to UNSUPPORTED/UNVERIFIED (L383-387). (3) The SET is aggregated only as a WEIGHT, never as a joint entailment: ClaimBasket (credibility_pass.py:210-229) keeps ALL supporting_members and carries weight_mass + corroboration. corroboration_count is computed over INDEPENDENT registrable-domains (finding_dedup.py:696-721, count_independent_hosts) with same-work de-padding (finding_dedup.py:455-546, consolidate_same_work) so N URLs of one paper count as ONE origin — and the module is explicit this is 'a Signal-D WEIGHT, never a gate' (finding_dedup.py:630). (4) Downstream, weighted_enrichment.select_unbound_supports_by_weight (weighted_enrichment.py:286-329) surfaces a basket member ONLY if its own span_verdict=='SUPPORTS' (L210-211), then ORDERS by relevance then weight_mass — keep-all-sort-below-floor-last, never a set-level verdict. So the whole-basket signal exists as a credibility WEIGHT and a max-over-members surfacing rule, but there is NO calibrated set-level entailment/sufficiency verdict that asks 'does this basket JOINTLY support the claim?'.
facets.gap_vs_frontier: The gap is per-member-isolation-plus-corroboration-WEIGHT vs a calibrated SET-LEVEL support/sufficiency VERDICT — and it is deliberate, not an omission. POLARIS's single-token anti-laundering invariant (credibility_pass.py:367-379) intentionally BLOCKS the naive frontier move of running NLI over the concatenated union of a basket (which can manufacture support no single source makes — evidence laundering). The frontier (MedRAGChecker's logistic-fused P* over the whole set; dual-perspective credibility-weighted aggregation with explicit source disagreement; natural-logic proof-based set SUFFICIENCY; AVeriTeC's Conflicting/Cherry-picking set-label) adds an ADVISORY set-level layer ON TOP of per-span verification: (a) a CALIBRATED basket-confidence that combines the per-member binary verdicts + corroboration weight + agreement into one probability (POLARIS today exposes binary span_verdict + an uncalibrated weight_mass, never a fused confidence); (b) an explicit DISAGREEMENT verdict when corroborators conflict (POLARIS has refuter_cluster_ids advisory data but no surfaced Conflicting/Cherry-picking basket label); (c) a faithful-by-construction SUFFICIENCY check (is the basket ENOUGH?). Because POLARIS's faithfulness engine (strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance) is FROZEN and untouchable, the only sovereign-correct improvement is a NEW advisory aggregation layer that consumes the existing per-member verdicts + weight_mass — it must NEVER concatenate spans into a joint-entailment gate (that re-opens laundering) and must NEVER relax the per-span gate. Sovereignty caveat: DeepTRACE (GPT-5), MedRAGChecker teacher (GPT-4.1/4o), RAGChecker/VeriScore default judges, and SAFE/FActScore are GPT-judge-dependent; HerO is CC-BY-NC. Any candidate must run on open-weight judges (the GLM-5.2 / Qwen / Llama-3.1 distilled-student route) to stay sovereign.

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS already consolidates at CLAIM-and-WORK level, ahead of the public stacks (STORM/GPT-Researcher dedup only at URL level). The basket primitive is `ClaimBasket` (src/polaris_graph/synthesis/credibility_pass.py:210) whose `supporting_members: list[BasketMember]` keeps ALL sources, never dropped (credibility_pass.py:227, docstring 213/146 "CONSOLIDATE, don't DROP"); baskets are built at credibility_pass.py:755 (`supporting_members=members`). Finding-level consolidation: `synthesis/finding_dedup.py:dedup_by_finding` (line 575) clusters rows by a conservative numeric `_finding_key` (subject/predicate/value/unit/dose/arm/endpoint — finding_dedup.py:381) into `FindingCluster` (line 549) carrying `corroboration_count` = independent registrable-domains (line 712), and under PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN BYPASSES the non-representative drop so every same-claim row flows through as a basket (collapsed_row_count honestly 0, line 737-742). Same-WORK de-padding: `consolidate_same_work` (finding_dedup.py:455) groups rows by normalized DOI else folded-title+discriminator (`_same_work_key` line 316) so N URLs of one paper count as ONE origin while KEEPING all member URLs/evidence_ids as corroborating locators (line 773-779), with the identical byte-for-byte key duplicated in `generator/weighted_enrichment.py:_work_identity` (line 922) for render-side multi-citation (`build_verified_span_draft` line 951 emits ALL same-work `[ev_id]` markers on one unit, line 1027-1031). Origin-cluster weighting: `synthesis/weight_mass.py:weight_mass` sums authority over INDEPENDENT origins so 50 copies count once (advisory, default-OFF, line 1-15). Cross-section restatement is handled in `generator/fact_dedup.py` (numeric signatures + a gated prose-Jaccard pass, line 450) via keep-all cross-ref rewrite (rewrite_redundant_sentences line 826, keep-all on failure line 876). Breadth surfacing: `weighted_enrichment.select_unbound_supports_by_weight` (line 286) offers the FULL ordered unbound-SUPPORTS basket members with the relevance floor demoted to an ORDERING weight, not a drop (B18, line 300-312).
facets.gap_vs_frontier: POLARIS is AHEAD of STORM/GPT-Researcher on this axis (they dedup only per-URL with one citation index per source — StormInformationTable.construct_url_to_info / _merge_new_info_to_references; GPT-Researcher only added URL-dedup) and has already adopted Argus-style keep-all baskets (weighted_enrichment.py is "I-arch-011 B18"; MEMORY notes "Argus keep-all baskets"). Two real gaps remain. ## Section 0 — How this report is grounded

This report synthesizes 7 facet researches into one map of the 2025/2026 best practice for the consolidation-and-baskets layer of POLARIS, and what to keep, add, or fix.

Two honesty notes up front, per LAW II.

First, I verified every load-bearing claim about the current POLARIS code against the repo myself. I read the actual files. ## Section 2 — THE BASKET PIPELINE: the ordered chain

This is the spine. Six steps, in order. For each step: the 2025/2026 SOTA method, the open-source pick, and the POLARIS plug-point.

### Step 1 — Atomic claims (from prose to checkable atoms)

- SOTA method: Claimify four stages — split, select only verifiable propositions, disambiguate and drop when ambiguity cannot be resolved, decompose into self-contained atoms. The drop-on-ambiguity rule matches the clinical never-fabricate posture.
- OSS pick: VeriScore (Apache-2.0) as a pre-filter for verifiable-versus-not, then CORE (MIT) for entailment-filtered atoms.
- POLARIS plug-point: `claim_atom_extractor.py`. Confirmed live — imported by `multi_section_generator.py`, `evidence_distiller.py`, `sentinel_contract.py`, and `atom_refusal_validator.py`. It is pure regex with a hardcoded clinical vocabulary and no LLM. The plug-in adds a span-faithful LLM decomposer beside it for out-of-vocabulary and non-clinical claims.

### Step 2 — Clustering into baskets (group same-claim sources)

- SOTA method: embed each decontextualized claim, then agglomerative or community-detection clustering at a distance threshold, keep-all members. MultiClaimNet recipe.
- OSS pick: Qwen3-Embedding-8B (Apache-2.0, already in-tree) plus sentence-transformers community detection. A hybrid lexical-plus-dense candidate generation, then an NLI same-claim confirm, to preserve the conservative never-over-merge rule.
- POLARIS plug-point: `finding_dedup.py` and `fact_dedup.py` group by literal numeric keys or exact content-word Jaccard. `claim_graph.py` is the field-agnostic upgrade already in-tree but default-off (`PG_SWEEP_CLAIM_GRAPH`, confirmed empty default). Swap its SHA-1 literal-key grouping for embedding-plus-NLI grouping.

### Step 3 — Corroboration and weighting (decide basket strength)

- SOTA method: reliability-aware truth discovery — a source's weight reflects its agreement with other credible sources, not just its venue. Plus a negative-signal domain down-weight.
- OSS pick: crowd-kit Dawid-Skene or M-MSR (Apache-2.0) for reliability-weighted consensus; CRED-1 (CC-BY-4.0) as a down-weight-only overlay for known-bad domains.
- POLARIS plug-point: `weight_mass.py` sums authority over independent origins, copies count zero — confirmed, and confirmed default-off with no production caller. `corroboration.py` counts independent registrable domains. The plug-in upgrades the flat count to a reliability-weighted consensus, weight-only, never adding to any count.

### Step 4 — Conflict detection (find disagreement inside a basket)

- SOTA method: run NLI over every basket pair to catch prose-only directional disagreement with no number and no assertion cue, then arbitrate by credibility weight.
- OSS pick: ModernCE-large-nli (MIT, local, 8192 context). Always-on, zero API spend, no socket-hang class.
- POLARIS plug-point: `semantic_conflict_detector.py` (under `retrieval/`, confirmed). It is a remote GLM-5.2 judge, default-off, hard-capped to 60 judged pairs and 200 rows. The frozen-engine constraint holds: this detector is advisory, it feeds labels and the both-sides composer, it never gates a sentence. ModernCE makes the capped optional pass an always-on local basket attribute.

### Step 5 — Set verification (does the whole basket support the claim?)

- SOTA method: a calibrated set-level support score that fuses per-member verdicts plus corroboration weight plus agreement into one probability, with an explicit conflicting or cherry-picking label.
- OSS pick: MiniCheck as the per-member primitive feeding a set aggregator; the dual-perspective credibility-weighted aggregation pattern for the calibrated confidence.
- POLARIS plug-point: `credibility_pass.py`, the `_verify_member_in_isolation` path. Critical constraint, verified at line 369: each member is verified alone, against its own span, with exactly one provenance token so the verifier cannot aggregate spans across members. This is the anti-laundering invariant. No frontier set-verifier may concatenate basket spans into a joint-entailment gate — that re-opens evidence laundering. The set layer is admissible only as an advisory confidence over already-isolated per-member verdicts.

### Step 6 — Synthesis and reduce (compose one grounded answer)

- SOTA method: hierarchical or iterative merge that preserves cross-source structure as the corpus grows, with conflict resolved at reduce time by confidence calibration. Not a flat single pass.
- OSS pick: CAHM (MIT) for the recursive re-grounded merge shape; LLMxMapReduce V1 (Apache-2.0) for the confidence-calibration reduce.
- POLARIS plug-point: `evidence_distiller.py`, gated by `PG_SECTION_DISTILL` (confirmed). The map step is per-source and fail-closed today. The reduce is a single-pass per-section compose, and conflict clustering uses exact-string `contradiction_key` equality (confirmed). Upgrade only the reduce shape and the conflict protocol. The reduce still feeds the frozen strict_verify; it never resolves conflict in place of it.

## Section 3 — KEEP vs ADD vs FIX against current POLARIS

Honest, weight-and-consolidate DNA, faithfulness engine frozen.

### KEEP — POLARIS is genuinely ahead here

- The keep-all basket primitive. `ClaimBasket` keeps every supporting member, never drops. This is ahead of the public deep-research stacks, which dedup only at URL level.
- Claim-level and work-level consolidation. `finding_dedup.py` keeps all same-claim rows as a basket under the redesign flag and de-pads same-work duplicates so N URLs of one paper count as one origin.
- The anti-laundering single-token isolation in `credibility_pass.py`. This is a correct, deliberate defense. Do not let any set-verifier override it.
- The frozen faithfulness engine. Untouched by everything in this report.
- The breadth-surfacing rule in `weighted_enrichment.py`: full ordered list, keep-all, sort-below-floor-last, no cap.

### ADD — the real gaps, advisory only

- Semantic and paraphrase clustering. This is the single biggest real gap. Today grouping is literal — numeric signatures or exact Jaccard. Two paraphrases of one clinical claim, for example "not recommended" versus "should be avoided" versus "contraindicated", never land in one basket, so corroboration is under-counted exactly where the DNA needs it. Add embedding-plus-NLI clustering.
- Calibrated basket confidence. Today a basket carries a 4-value label and an uncalibrated weight. Add a Beta-Binomial or logistic-fused posterior that turns a basket into a calibrated probability, weighting members already in the basket, never adding to a count.
- Reliability-aware weighting. Today source weight is a fixed authority prior. Add a reliability-weighted consensus so weight reflects agreement with other credible sources.
- A surfaced conflict label. Today disagreement is implied by the both-sides block. Add an explicit conflicting or cherry-picking attribute on the basket.

### FIX — verified, repo-grounded, highest confidence, do these first

- The flag split-brain. Verified directly: `credibility_pass.py:118` reads `getenv(PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN, "on")` — default ON in synthesis. `live_retriever.py:379` reads the same flag with an empty default and an in-set test — default OFF in retrieval. So on an unset run, the down-weight-don't-drop redesign is live in synthesis but the retriever still hard-drops content-starved rows. This is the same funnel class as the I-arch-007 collapse. Make the retriever default match synthesis.
- Wire the dead modules. All verified as default-off or no-production-caller: `calibration_metrics.py` (Brier, ECE, MCE — pure offline, zero callers), `both_sides.py` (`PG_SWEEP_BOTHSIDES_DISCLOSURE`, no caller), `weight_mass.py` (`PG_SWEEP_WEIGHT_MASS`, no caller), `claim_graph.py` (`PG_SWEEP_CLAIM_GRAPH`, default-off). The machinery exists; it is built but not rendered into the report path.
- Remove or flag `atomic_decomposer.py`. Verified: its `initialize()` falls back to `GeminiClient` at lines 133-134, a sovereignty violation. It is dead on the live path — only `auditor_agent.py` (pipeline-C) and `schemas.py` reference it. Flag it for removal.
